forecast-period.text: A ridge of high pressure will begin to extend from the west on Tuesday and will be briefly be weakened as a cold front crosses the southeast on Wednesday afternoon. The ridge will begin to re-establish in the wake of the front and stabilise over SA with the slow moving high pressure centre sitting south of WA by Friday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.text: West to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots tending west to northwesterly during the afternoon and evening. Winds reaching up to 20 knots south of Port MacDonnell in the late evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Wednesday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 15 to 25 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 25 knots in the middle of the day. Winds reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
1 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Port MacDonnell in the evening. Possible small hail in the late afternoon and evening.
Cloudy. 70% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning. Possible small hail in the morning.
